import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ line: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(line)
  mut cur = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    let bopt = string.byte_at(line, i)
    match bopt
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
        if has
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
          else
            result = list.push(result, cur)
          end
          cur = 0
          has = false
          neg = false
        end
      else if b == 45 and not has
        neg = true
      else if b >= 48 and b <= 57
        cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
    else
      result = list.push(result, cur)
    end
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)
let trimmed = string.trim(line2)

let k = match string.parse_int(trimmed)
when Some(x)
  x
when None
  0
end

let total = list.len(nums)
let take = if k > total
  total
else
  k
end

mut used: List<Int> = []
mut output = ""
mut count = 0
while count < take
  mut best_idx = 0 - 1
  mut best_val = 0
  mut i = 0
  while i < total
    if not list.contains(used, i)
      let v = match list.get(nums, i)
      when Some(x)
        x
      when None
        0
      end
      if best_idx < 0
        best_idx = i
        best_val = v
      else if v > best_val
        best_idx = i
        best_val = v
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  used = list.push(used, best_idx)
  if count == 0
    output = "#{best_val}"
  else
    output = "#{output} #{best_val}"
  end
  count = count + 1
end

println(output)
